task_completion.description: Navigate to CPSC Recalls database or SaferProducts.gov
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Firefox browser windows displaying the CPSC Recalls database (cpsc.gov/Recalls) or SaferProducts.gov.

task_completion.description: Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Product Safety Research'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Firefox Library window, bookmarks sidebar, or bookmarks menu showing a folder exactly named 'Product Safety Research'.

task_completion.description: Save at least 6 relevant bookmarks in the folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the 'Product Safety Research' bookmark folder to verify it contains at least 6 saved bookmarks related to the product categories.

task_completion.description: Create the JSON risk assessment report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor, IDE, or terminal showing a file named 'cpsc_risk_assessment.json' saved in the '~/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Include the three required product categories in the JSON
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the JSON content to verify the presence of the top-level keys: 'space_heaters', 'power_banks', and 'childrens_sleepwear'.

task_completion.description: Populate at least 2 specific recalls for each category
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the JSON content to verify each category has a 'recalls_found' list with at least 2 objects containing actual data for 'recall_number', 'product_name', 'hazard', 'recall_date', and 'manufacturer'.

task_completion.description: Include common hazards and risk level for each category
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the JSON content to verify 'common_hazards' (a list of strings) and 'risk_level' (high, medium, or low) are filled out for each of the three categories.

integrity.description: Agent used the Firefox browser to research recalls
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that screenshots show Firefox being actively used to search and browse cpsc.gov or saferproducts.gov, rather than the agent hallucinating data without browsing.

integrity.description: Agent did not copy-paste placeholder values from the prompt
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the JSON file to ensure the agent replaced the literal string '...' from the prompt with actual research data for product names, hazards, manufacturers, etc.

integrity.description: Recall numbers follow the genuine CPSC format
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the recall numbers in the JSON match the YY-XXXXX format (e.g., 24-071) and are not left as the prompt's placeholder 'XX-XXX' or completely fabricated formats.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/cpsc_risk_assessment.json. The JSON must include exactly these categories: 'space_heaters', 'power_banks', and 'childrens_sleepwear'. Each category must have at least 2 recalls (6 total minimum). Recall numbers must match the regex ^\d{2}-\d{3,}$ (e.g., 24-123). The Firefox bookmark folder must be named 'Product Safety Research' and contain at least 6 bookmarks from cpsc.gov or saferproducts.gov.
